name: sec-review
description: Cybersecurity review plugin for web services, servers, browser extensions, Rust projects, Android apps, iOS apps, desktop Linux, desktop macOS, and desktop Windows: a pipelined thirteen-agent architecture (sec-expert, finding-triager, cve-enricher, sast-runner, dast-runner, webext-runner, rust-runner, android-runner, ios-runner, linux-runner, macos-runner, windows-runner, report-writer) with model pinning — sonnet-grade code reasoning, haiku-grade CVE feed I/O and eight-lane tool dispatch. Citation-grounded expertise across databases, frameworks, webservers (incl. Windows/IIS as of v0.4.0), proxies, frontend, browser extensions (v0.6.0), Rust/Cargo (v0.7.0), Android (v0.8.0), iOS (v0.9.0), desktop Linux (v0.10.0), desktop macOS (v0.11.0), desktop Windows (PE hardening + Authenticode + AppLocker/WDAC + MSI/MSIX, v0.12.0), auth, TLS, containers, secrets, and supply-chain; live CVE enrichment from NVD 2.0, OSV.dev (native for crates.io / Maven / NuGet; best-effort via GHSA for CocoaPods / SwiftPM / Debian), and GHSA with CISA KEV cross-reference; SAST (semgrep + bandit); DAST (OWASP ZAP baseline); webext (addons-linter + web-ext + retire.js); Rust (cargo-audit + cargo-deny + cargo-geiger INFO-ceiling + cargo-vet); Android (mobsfscan + apkleaks + android-lint); iOS (mobsfscan + Apple codesign/spctl/notarytool on macOS hosts); Linux desktop (systemd-analyze + lintian + checksec on systemd hosts with preconditioned targets); macOS desktop (mobsfscan + codesign + spctl + pkgutil + stapler on macOS hosts with .app/.framework/.pkg/.dmg targets); Windows desktop (binskim + osslsigncode cross-platform, sigcheck Windows-host-only, against .exe/.dll/.msi/.msix PE artifacts) — with origin-tagged findings, three-state sentinels, and a structured skipped-list primitive distinguishing clean skips (target-shape: no-apk/no-bundle/no-pkg/no-debian-source/no-elf/no-systemd-unit/no-pe; host-OS-gated: requires-macos-host/requires-systemd-host/requires-windows-host; profile-absent: no-notary-profile) from tool failures; per-run token-cost accounting; offline-degradation drills for CVE feeds and all eight tool lanes; prioritized markdown report with reliable, primary-source-cited fixes.
